description: Complete bytecode encoding tables for translating ARMv7-A assembly into machine code without an external assembler. All ARM mode instructions are 32-bit (4 bytes) wide, fixed-length. Covers all instructions generated by the SASM translator for ARM32 targets, plus the full set of commonly used ARMv7-A instructions for future expansion.
source_references: ARM Architecture Reference Manual ARMv7-A and ARMv7-R edition (DDI 0406C.d)
ARM ARM Section A8: Instruction Encoding

notes.instruction_width: All ARM mode instructions are exactly 32 bits (4 bytes). Thumb/Thumb-2 mode uses mixed 16/32-bit but is not covered here.
notes.byte_order: ARM uses little-endian byte ordering by default. A 32-bit instruction word 0xE3A01005 is stored in memory as bytes 05, 10, A0, E3.
notes.condition_codes: Bits [31:28] of every ARM instruction encode a condition code. Most instructions default to AL (0xE = always). Conditional execution avoids branches.
notes.encoding_format: ARM instructions use fixed bit-field positions. Unlike x86, there is no variable-length opcode or ModRM byte. The instruction format is determined by bits [27:25] and other fixed fields.

encoding_types.DPI: Data Processing Immediate — format: cond[31:28] | 001 | opcode[24:21] | S[20] | Rn[19:16] | Rd[15:12] | rotate_imm[11:8] | imm8[7:0]
encoding_types.DPR: Data Processing Register — format: cond[31:28] | 000 | opcode[24:21] | S[20] | Rn[19:16] | Rd[15:12] | shift[11:4] | Rm[3:0]
encoding_types.DPS: Data Processing Register-shifted Register — format: cond[31:28] | 000 | opcode[24:21] | S[20] | Rn[19:16] | Rd[15:12] | Rs[11:8] | 0 | shift_type[6:5] | 1 | Rm[3:0]
encoding_types.LSI: Load/Store Immediate offset — format: cond[31:28] | 01 | I[25]=0 | P[24] | U[23] | B[22] | W[21] | L[20] | Rn[19:16] | Rt[15:12] | imm12[11:0]
encoding_types.LSR: Load/Store Register offset — format: cond[31:28] | 01 | I[25]=1 | P[24] | U[23] | B[22] | W[21] | L[20] | Rn[19:16] | Rt[15:12] | shift[11:4] | Rm[3:0]
encoding_types.LSM: Load/Store Multiple — format: cond[31:28] | 100 | P[24] | U[23] | S[22] | W[21] | L[20] | Rn[19:16] | register_list[15:0]
encoding_types.BRX: Branch and Exchange — format: cond[31:28] | 00010010 | SBO[19:8] | 0001 | Rm[3:0]
encoding_types.MSR: Move to Status Register — various sub-formats

operand_notation.Rd: Destination register (4-bit code, bits [15:12] in most data processing)
operand_notation.Rt: Transfer register for load/store (4-bit code, bits [15:12])
operand_notation.Ra: Accumulate register in multiply (4-bit code, bits [15:12])
operand_notation.imm24: 24-bit signed offset for branches (bits [23:0]), shifted left 2, PC-relative
operand_notation.shift: Shift specifier: shift_type[6:5] + shift_amount[11:7] or Rs[11:8]
operand_notation.register_list: 16-bit bitmask of registers for LDM/STM (bit N = register N)

CS.description: Carry set / unsigned higher or same

instruction_format_bits.description: ARM instruction encoding is determined by bits [27:25] (and sometimes [27:20] or further). This table maps the top-level encoding categories.

categories.category: Data Processing (immediate) / Move Immediate to Status Register
Coprocessor Data Processing / Coprocessor Register Transfer / SVC

forms.description: Rd = Rn AND operand2 — register
ANDS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn AND operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn AND operand2 — immediate
Rd = Rn XOR operand2 — register
EORS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn XOR operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn XOR operand2 — immediate
Rd = Rn - operand2 — register
SUBS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn - operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn - operand2 — immediate
Rd = operand2 - Rn — register
RSBS — register, set flags
Rd = operand2 - Rn — register with shifted operand
Rd = operand2 - Rn — immediate
Rd = Rn + operand2 — register
ADDS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn + operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn + operand2 — immediate
Rd = Rn + operand2 + C — register
ADCS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn + operand2 + C — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn + operand2 + C — immediate
Rd = Rn - operand2 - !C — register
SBCS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn - operand2 - !C — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn - operand2 - !C — immediate
Rd = operand2 - Rn - !C — register
RSCS — register, set flags
Rd = operand2 - Rn - !C — register with shifted operand
Rd = operand2 - Rn - !C — immediate
Rn AND operand2 (flags only) — register, set flags
Rn AND operand2 (flags only) — register with shifted Rm
Rn AND operand2 (flags only) — immediate
Rn XOR operand2 (flags only) — register, set flags
Rn XOR operand2 (flags only) — register with shifted Rm
Rn XOR operand2 (flags only) — immediate
Rn - operand2 (flags only) — register, set flags
Rn - operand2 (flags only) — register with shifted Rm
Rn - operand2 (flags only) — immediate
Rn + operand2 (flags only) — register, set flags
Rn + operand2 (flags only) — register with shifted Rm
Rn + operand2 (flags only) — immediate
Rd = Rn OR operand2 — register
ORRS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn OR operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn OR operand2 — immediate
Rd = operand2 — register
MOVS — register, set flags
Rd = operand2 — register with shifted Rm
Rd = operand2 — immediate
Rd = Rn AND NOT operand2 — register
BICS — register, set flags
Rd = Rn AND NOT operand2 — register with shifted operand
Rd = Rn AND NOT operand2 — immediate
Rd = NOT operand2 — register
MVNS — register, set flags
Rd = NOT operand2 — register with shifted Rm
Rd = NOT operand2 — immediate
Rd = Rm * Rs (low 32 bits)
MULS — multiply, set flags
Rd = (Rm * Rs) + Ra — Multiply-Accumulate
RdHi:RdLo = Rm * Rs (unsigned 64-bit result)
RdHi:RdLo = Rm * Rs (signed 64-bit result)
Rd = Rn / Rm (signed divide, ARMv7-A with IDIV extension)
Rd = Rn / Rm (unsigned divide, ARMv7-A with IDIV extension)
Load word: Rt = [Rn + imm12]
Load word: Rt = [Rn + Rm]
Load word post-indexed: Rt = [Rn], Rn += imm12
Load word pre-indexed with write-back: Rt = [Rn + imm12], Rn += imm12

imm12.note: Offset from PC to literal pool entry

forms.description: Load word from literal pool (PC-relative): Rt = [PC + offset_to_literal]
Load unsigned byte: Rt = zero_extend([Rn + imm12])
Load unsigned halfword: Rt = zero_extend_16([Rn + imm8])
Load signed byte: Rt = sign_extend([Rn + imm8])
Load signed halfword: Rt = sign_extend_16([Rn + imm8])
Store word: [Rn + imm12] = Rt
Store word: [Rn + Rm] = Rt
Store word post-indexed: [Rn] = Rt, Rn += imm12
Store word pre-indexed with write-back: [Rn + imm12] = Rt, Rn += imm12
Store byte: [Rn + imm12] = Rt[7:0]
Store halfword: [Rn + imm8] = Rt[15:0]

register_list.note: Bitmask: bit N = 1 means push rN

forms.description: Push registers to stack: STMDB SP!, {register_list}

register_list.note: Bitmask: bit N = 1 means pop rN

forms.description: Pop registers from stack: LDMIA SP!, {register_list}
Load multiple (increment after): LDMIA Rn, {register_list}
Load multiple with write-back: LDMIA Rn!, {register_list}
Store multiple (increment after): STMIA Rn, {register_list}
Store multiple with write-back: STMIA Rn!, {register_list}
Branch unconditional: PC = PC + offset

imm24.note: Signed offset >> 2. LR = address of next instruction, PC = PC + 8 + (sign_extend(imm24) << 2)

forms.description: Branch with link (function call): LR = return address, PC = PC + offset
Branch and exchange: PC = Rm. If Rm[0]=1, switch to Thumb mode. BX LR = return from function.
Branch with link and exchange: LR = return address, PC = Rm, may switch to Thumb
Conditional branch (BEQ): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BNE): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BCS): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BCC): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BMI): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BPL): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BVS): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BVC): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BHI): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BLS): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BGE): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BLT): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BGT): if condition, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch (BLE): if condition, PC = PC + offset

imm24.note: Immediate value passed to supervisor (Linux uses 0 and passes syscall number in r7)

forms.description: Supervisor Call (formerly SWI): trigger software interrupt for OS system call
Software Interrupt (old name for SVC)
No operation: encoded as MOV r0, r0
Move wide: Rd[15:0] = imm16, Rd[31:16] = 0. For loading 16-bit constants.
Move top: Rd[31:16] = imm16, Rd[15:0] unchanged. Use MOVW+MOVT to load any 32-bit constant.
Count Leading Zeros
Move from CPSR to register
Move register to CPSR fields
Software breakpoint
Data Memory Barrier
Data Synchronization Barrier
Instruction Synchronization Barrier
Wait For Interrupt
Wait For Event
Send Event
Yield hint
Load word exclusive (for atomic operations)
Store word exclusive (for atomic operations). Rd = 0 if store succeeded.
Sign extend byte: Rd = sign_extend(Rm[7:0])
Sign extend halfword: Rd = sign_extend(Rm[15:0])
Zero extend byte: Rd = Rm[7:0]
Zero extend halfword: Rd = Rm[15:0]
LSL Rd, Rm, #imm5 — encoded as MOV Rd, Rm, LSL #imm5
LSL Rd, Rm, Rs — shift by register amount
LSR Rd, Rm, #imm5 — encoded as MOV Rd, Rm, LSR #imm5
LSR Rd, Rm, Rs — shift by register amount
ASR Rd, Rm, #imm5 — encoded as MOV Rd, Rm, ASR #imm5
ASR Rd, Rm, Rs — shift by register amount
ROR Rd, Rm, #imm5 — encoded as MOV Rd, Rm, ROR #imm5
ROR Rd, Rm, Rs — shift by register amount
Rotate Right with Extend: Rd = (C << 31) | (Rm >> 1). Encoded as MOV Rd, Rm, ROR #0

Rm.note: Rm not used — immediate form with imm=0

forms.description: Negate: Rd = 0 - Rm. Encoded as RSB Rd, Rm, #0

data_directives.description: Reserve N bytes (filled with optional fill value, default 0)
Repeat a value: .fill count, size, value

condition_code_aliases.description: Unsigned higher or same (same as CS)

branch_aliases.description: Branch if unsigned higher or same (same as BCS)
Branch if unsigned lower (same as BCC)
